texto: It appears - because it has been the case for twenty years - that every problem is solvable...that no matter how badly the world economy slumps there is a pain-free way out of it. Once the realization dawns that there is not, and that the pain will be severe, the question is posed that has not really been posed for twenty years: who should feel it?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Paul Mason que provoca temas ligados a familia. Ao questionar “It appears - because it has been the case for twenty years - that every problem is solvable...tha…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, austerity, capitalism, com foco em capitalism e economics.
